name: Iriepal and the viewpoints of Santo Verde and Balcon de la Pena Hueva — circular trailrunning from Guadalajara
name_original: Iriépal y los miradores del Santo Verde y del Balcón de la Peña Hueva — circular trailrunning desde Guadalajara
region_intro: Jogging routes around Campiña traverse a diverse landscape, offering varied terrain for runners. The region features areas like Los Cerros Natural Park, characterized by dry streams, limestone cliffs, and pine forests, providing challenging trails with elevation changes. Additionally, the Vía Verde de la Campiña offers flatter, accessible routes through rolling countryside, cultivated fields, and olive groves, often following river valleys. These environments combine to create a range of running experiences across the Campiña region. Best jogging routes around Campiña The most popular jogging route is Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva — circular trailrunning through the Cañada de la Caleriza, a 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This challenging route traverses varied terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Iriepal and the viewpoints of Santo Verde and Balcon de la Pena Hueva — circular trailrunning from Guadalajara, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This route offers panoramic views and diverse natural scenery. Local runners also love the Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 33 minutes. Jogging around Campiña is defined by limestone cliffs, rolling agricultural plains, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging trail running. The routes in Campiña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1800 runners have used komoot to explore Campiña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Campiña region?
A: The Campiña region offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 150 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy paths to challenging trail runs, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Campiña?
A: Yes, Campiña has several easy routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032426567/molino-de-vinuelas-circular-from-vinuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) trail that can be completed in about 33 minutes, leading through varied landscapes. Overall, there are 16 easy routes available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Campiña?
A: The Campiña region offers diverse terrain. In areas like the Campiña near Alcalá de Henares, you'll find trails traversing dry streams, impressive limestone cliffs, and pine forests, providing challenging routes with elevation changes. In the Campiña in Andalusia, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1771204/running-trails-around-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt; offers flatter, accessible routes through rolling countryside, cultivated fields, and olive groves, often following river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options in Campiña?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Campiña offers difficult routes with significant elevation gains. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031425963/presa-de-la-parra-from-ponton-de-la-oliva-circular-trailrunning-through-the-canada-de-la-caleriza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva — circular trailrunning through the Cañada de la Caleriza&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.3-mile (16.6 km) route with varied terrain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030876300/iriepal-and-the-viewpoints-of-santo-verde-and-balcon-de-la-pena-hueva-circular-trailrunning-from-guadalajara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iriepal and the viewpoints of Santo Verde and Balcon de la Pena Hueva — circular trailrunning from Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.1-mile (16.3 km) path offering panoramic views.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while running in Campiña?
A: Many routes in Campiña offer beautiful sights. You can enjoy panoramic views from the Ecce-Homo summit in Los Cerros Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Hueva loop from Restaurante Las Galeras&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas. Along the Vía Verde de la Campiña, you'll see the Guadalquivir River valley and cross the Guadajoz River. Other natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5605308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3151079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Henares River from the natural overlook&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, the Campiña region is rich in history. Near Alcalá de Henares, some routes incorporate historical sites like the Alcalá la Vieja Fortress. Along the Vía Verde de la Campiña, you can see the impressive Baroque bell towers of Écija and the distant view of the castle in Almodóvar del Río. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam and Cliffside Pathways&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Mill&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3405880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Infantado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Campiña?
A: For long-distance runners, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1771204/running-trails-around-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt; in Andalusia is an excellent choice. It's Spain's longest railway path, stretching 91 km (56.5 miles) and offering a relatively flat and accessible route through fertile plains and olive groves. Another longer route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Ring – Palacio del Infantado loop from Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.17 km (9.4 miles).

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Campiña?
A: The running routes in Campiña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from limestone cliffs to rolling agricultural plains, and the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Campiña?
A: Yes, many routes in Campiña are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del géiser loop from Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33728887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henares River viewpoint – Green Ring loop from Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Campiña?
A: The Campiña region is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be hot, especially in the Andalusian Campiña, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winter offers cooler temperatures, which can be ideal for longer runs, though some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any running routes accessible by public transport in Campiña?
A: While specific public transport details for each route vary, many towns and cities within the Campiña region, such as Alcalá de Henares and Guadalajara, are well-connected by public transport. Routes starting from these urban centers, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24544624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;, are often easily accessible.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in Campiña?
A: Yes, some routes are suitable for families, especially those looking for less strenuous options. The Vía Verde de la Campiña, with its relatively flat and well-maintained path, is a great choice for families. Look for routes marked as 'easy' in the komoot guide, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5319259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Azuqueca de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.9 km (4.9 miles) route with minimal elevation change.
tips: Viewpoint located at the top of the place called Cuesta de Lucena
The viewpoint located in Peña Hueva is a recreational space for walks and public use for many citizens of the city of Guadalajara. It offers very beautiful views of Alcarria.
